title: Medical and dental technicians
description: Promote health within a community by assisting individuals to adopt healthy behaviors. Serve as an advocate for the health needs of individuals by assisting community residents in effectively communicating with healthcare providers or social service agencies. Act as liaison or advocate and implement programs that promote, maintain, and improve individual and overall community health. May deliver health-related preventive services such as blood pressure, glaucoma, and hearing screenings. May collect data to help identify community health needs. Provide healthcare services typically performed by a physician, under the supervision of a physician. Conduct complete physicals, provide treatment, and counsel patients. May, in some cases, prescribe medication. Must graduate from an accredited educational program for physician assistants. Assist anesthesiologists in the administration of anesthesia for surgical and non-surgical procedures. Monitor patient status and provide patient care during surgical treatment. (Based on 39 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare cell samples by applying special staining techniques, such as chromosomal staining, to differentiate cells or cell components. Prepare slides of cell cultures following standard procedures. Harvest cell cultures using substances such as mitotic arrestants, cell releasing agents, and cell fixatives. Prepare biological specimens such as amniotic fluids, bone marrow, tumors, chorionic villi, and blood for chromosome examinations.

Select appropriate methods of preparation and storage of media to maintain potential of hydrogen (pH), sterility, or ability to support growth. Identify appropriate methods of specimen collection, preservation, or transport. Maintain effective laboratory operations by adhering to standards of specimen collection, preparation, or laboratory safety. Summarize test results and report to appropriate authorities. Verify availability of operating room supplies, medications, and gases.

Conduct quality control checks on laboratory equipment or cameras. Establish or monitor quality assurance programs or activities to ensure the accuracy of laboratory results. Input details of specimens into logs or computer systems. Document specimens by verifying patients' and specimens' information. Archive diagnostic material, such as histologic slides and blocks. Maintain laboratory equipment, such as microscopes, mass spectrometers, microtomes, immunostainers, tissue processors, embedding centers, and water baths. Operate or oversee operation of radiologic or magnetic imaging equipment to produce images of the body for diagnostic purposes. Cut sections of body tissues for microscopic examination, using microtomes.

Provide patient clinical data or microscopic findings to assist pathologists in the preparation of pathology reports. Assist pathologists or other physicians to collect cell samples by fine needle aspiration (FNA) biopsy or other methods. Examine specimens, using microscopes, to evaluate specimen quality. Examine cell samples to detect abnormalities in the color, shape, or size of cellular components and patterns. Recognize and report abnormalities in the color, size, shape, composition, or pattern of cells.

Provide airway management interventions including tracheal intubation, fiber optics, or ventilatory support. Monitor patient status and provide patient care during surgical treatment. Respond to emergency situations by providing cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) or other life support techniques such as advanced cardiac life support (ACLS). Collect and document patients' pre-anesthetic health histories. Monitor and document patients' progress during the post-anesthesia period. Assist anesthesiologists in monitoring of patients, including electrocardiogram (EKG), blood pressure, and routine measurement of temperature, respiration, and heart rate.

Conduct and evaluate hearing tests including air conduction, bone conduction, or speech audiometry tests. Train clients to use hearing aids or other augmentative communication devices. Create or modify impressions for earmolds and hearing aid shells. Maintain or repair hearing aids or other communication devices. Search and observe patients to ensure safety and comfort during procedures.

Instruct patients in the use and care of orthoses and prostheses. Fit, test, and evaluate devices on patients and make adjustments for proper fit, function, and comfort. Fabricate orthopedic and prosthetic devices based on physician prescriptions and the measurement of patients. Train and supervise staff in the construction and fitting of orthopedic and prosthetic devices. Repair, rebuild, and modify prosthetic and orthopedic appliances as needed. Design and construct orthotic or prosthetic devices, using materials such as thermoplastic, metal alloys, and leather.

Prepare radiopharmaceuticals or contrast material under the direction of a physician for diagnostic procedures. Operate and monitor equipment used in imaging to ensure optimal functioning. Review and evaluate developed x-rays and imaging data to determine if images meet diagnostic needs. Position patient for imaging and adjust equipment for optimum views. Report abnormalities or issues to the supervising physician.

Provide clinical services or health education to improve and maintain the oral health of patients or the general public. Maintain dental equipment and sharpen and sterilize dental instruments. Examine gums, using probes, to locate periodontal recessed gums and signs of gum disease.

Collect samples, such as blood, urine, or sputum from patients, and perform routine laboratory tests on samples. Examine slides under microscopes to ensure tissue preparation meets laboratory requirements. Operate, calibrate, or maintain equipment used in quantitative or qualitative analysis, such as spectrophotometers, calorimeters, or computer-controlled analyzers.

Conduct screening interviews of patients to identify contraindications, such as ferrous objects, pregnancy, prosthetic heart valves, cardiac pacemakers, or tattoos. Measure and record patients' vital signs, such as height, weight, temperature, blood pressure, pulse, or respiration. Verify and document patient medical histories.

Administer or order diagnostic tests, such as x-ray, electrocardiogram, and laboratory tests. Obtain, compile, and record patient medical data, including health history, progress notes, and results of physical examinations. Analyze the results of tests or experiments to ensure conformity to specifications.

Operate digital picture archiving communications systems. Archive case documentation and study materials as required by regulations and laws. Maintain records, reports, or files as required, including such information as radiation dosages, equipment settings, or patients' reactions.

Monitor patient status and provide patient care during surgical treatment. Assist anesthesiologists in the administration of anesthesia for surgical and non-surgical procedures. Help physicians, radiation oncologists, or clinical physicists to prepare physical or technical aspects of radiation treatment plans, using information about patient condition and anatomy.

Educate, prepare, and reassure patients and their families by answering questions, providing physical assistance, and reinforcing physicians' advice regarding treatment reactions or post-treatment care. Instruct and counsel patients about prescribed therapeutic regimens, normal growth and development, family planning, emotional problems of daily living, and health maintenance.

Act as liaison with physicist and supportive care personnel. Collaborate with other healthcare personnel as part of a healthcare team to assess patient needs, plan and modify care, and implement interventions. Supervise and coordinate activities of technicians and technical assistants.
Analyze laboratory findings to check the accuracy of the results. Prepare patients for examinations, tests, or treatments and explain procedures. Record food and fluid intake and output.
Schedule patients for treatment times. Schedule appointments for research subjects or clinical patients. Order medical and laboratory supplies and equipment.

Prepare or examine food trays for conformance to prescribed diet. Provide medical treatment or personal care to patients in private home settings, such as cooking, keeping rooms orderly, seeing that patients are comfortable and in good spirits, or instructing family members in simple nursing tasks.
Help patients with bathing, dressing, maintaining personal hygiene, moving in bed, or standing and walking. Answer patients' calls and determine how to assist them.

Perform therapeutic procedures, such as injections, immunizations, suturing and wound care, and infection management. Apply compresses, ice bags, or hot water bottles. Maintain patient recall system and manage appointments.

Prepare standard volumetric solutions or reagents to be combined with samples, following standardized formulas or experimental procedures. Collect blood or tissue samples from patients, observing principles of asepsis to obtain blood sample.

Supervise, train, or direct lab assistants, medical and clinical laboratory technicians or technologists, or other medical laboratory workers engaged in laboratory testing. Perform clerical duties, such as scheduling exams or special procedures, keeping records, or archiving computerized images.
Maintain and calibrate radioisotope and laboratory equipment. Install, clean, and maintain imaging equipment; conduct quality assurance processes to ensure accurate operation.

Develop, implement, and monitor quality control and quality assurance programs to ensure accurate and precise test performance and reports. Troubleshoot technical issues related to magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanner or peripheral equipment.
Document and interpret diagnostic test results. Conduct chemical analyses of body fluids, such as blood or urine, to detect abnormalities or diseases and enter findings into a computer.

Provide basic patient care or treatments, such as taking temperatures or blood pressures, dressing wounds, treating bedsores. Observe patients, charting and reporting changes in patients' conditions, such as adverse reactions to medication or treatment, and taking any necessary action.
Gather information on patients' illnesses and medical history to guide the choice of diagnostic procedures for therapy, including taking and documenting patients' medical histories and vital signs.
Maintain stock and supplies, prepare supplies for special examinations, and order supplies when necessary.
Supervise or train students or other medical technicians, including student nuclear medicine technologists and medical sonographers.
Develop treatment procedures and plans for medical treatments, including nuclear medicine treatment programs and radiation treatment in consultation with the oncology team.
Administer local anesthetic agents and assist in management of medical or dental emergencies.
Provide basic health services, such as first aid, and perform legal and ethical duties, including obtaining consent from patients for invasive procedures.
Monitor nutritional health of children, elderly, or other high-risk groups, and conduct nutritional assessments, including obtaining and evaluating dietary histories.
Assist patients during examinations or procedures, ensuring their safety and comfort, and instruct them in self-care practices, including oral hygiene.
Record treatment information in patient records and maintain updated client records with plans, notes, and quality assurance records.
Advise community groups about health services and issues related to improving general health, including sanitation, nutrition, and preventative measures.
Contact clients to ensure they have completed health-related actions, and refer community members to needed health services.
Conduct community outreach, including distributing educational materials and teaching health information to community groups.
Conduct diagnostic procedures and screenings, such as blood pressure screenings and other health assessments to evaluate community health needs.
Clean teeth, using dental instruments, and assist in dental procedures by sterilizing instruments and preparing materials.
Prepare patients for examinations, including explaining procedures and ensuring proper positioning and comfort during diagnostic tests.
Develop plans or contracts for individuals or community groups to improve health outcomes.
Collect information from individuals to compile vital statistics about the general health of community members.
Measure and record diagnostic results, using testing techniques and equipment, including ultrasound machines and other medical instrumentation.
Contact clients in person, by phone, or in writing to discuss health matters or follow up on public health interventions.
Develop and implement community health programs, acting as an advocate for individual or community health needs.
Attend community meetings or health fairs to understand community issues or build relationships with community members.

Work under the direction of occupational therapists to plan, implement, or administer educational, vocational, or recreational programs that restore or enhance performance in individuals with functional impairments. Attend care plan meetings to review patient progress and update care plans. Participate in research projects, conferences, or technical meetings. Observe patients' vital signs to assess physical condition. Order surgical supplies, maintain files and records of surgical procedures, clean and restock operating room, and prepare patients for surgery including positioning them on the operating table and covering them with sterile surgical drapes. Maintain a proper sterile field during surgical procedures and monitor patients' blood pressure and heart rate during diagnostic or therapeutic procedures. Monitor patients' comfort and safety during tests, alerting physicians to abnormalities or changes in patient responses. Explain testing procedures to patients to obtain cooperation and reduce anxiety.

Conduct electrocardiogram (EKG), phonocardiogram, echocardiogram, stress testing, or other cardiovascular tests to record patients' cardiac activity. Prepare and position patients for testing. Obtain and record patient identification, medical history, or test results. Attach electrodes to the patients' chests, arms, and legs, connect electrodes to leads from the EKG machine, and operate the machine to obtain readings. Adjust equipment and controls according to physicians' orders or established protocol. Submit reports to physicians summarizing test results. Monitor patients during tests using electroencephalographs (EEG) or other instruments to assess brain or nervous system activity. Collect and report data on topics, such as patient encounters or inter-institutional problems, making recommendations for change when appropriate. Test patients' hemoglobin, hematocrit, and blood glucose levels. Count sponges, needles, and instruments before and after operation, and provide emergency care during life-threatening situations.

Develop and implement treatment plans designed to help clients function independently, assist in training technicians or medical staff, and monitor patients' performance in therapy activities, providing encouragement. Explain policies, procedures, or services to patients using medical or administrative knowledge. Provide nursing, psychiatric, or personal care to patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities, and aid patients in performing essential tasks, such as bathing, dressing, or grooming themselves. Monitor patients' physical and emotional well-being and report unusual behavior or physical ailments to medical staff. Encourage patients to develop work skills and engage in social, recreational, or therapeutic activities. Assist educational specialists or clinical psychologists in administering tests to assess client abilities or progress. Collaborate with doctors or rehabilitation therapists in the treatment and rehabilitation of patients. Maintain communication between patients, family members, medical staff, and administrative staff to ensure comprehensive care.

Prepare and apply sterile wound dressings, assist in applying casts or splints, and monitor and maintain aseptic technique throughout procedures. Operate sterilizing devices and assist with patient resuscitation during life-threatening events. Ensure unobstructed operative fields using surgical retractors and other support tools as needed. Assist with volume replacement and monitor patient intra-operative status, including vital signs and other parameters.

Maintain documentation of all patients' contacts, review and update records as necessary. Provide information about community health and social resources, and recognize and respond to patients' health needs through advocacy and effective communication with healthcare providers. Collaborate in research studies to contribute to community health initiatives and assist with the development of educational materials for patients. Maintain knowledge of community services and resources available to patients and refer patients to appropriate health care services or resources when necessary. Communicate findings and recommendations to medical staff to inform patient care.

Collect patients' medical information needed to customize tests and provide information about physical and emotional processes involved in pregnancy, labor, birth, and postpartum periods. Monitor fetal growth and well-being, perform postpartum health assessments, and support patients with nutrition and care during pregnancy. Suture perineal lacerations and treat symptoms with alternative healthcare methods when suitable. Identify and monitor pregnancy-related problems and provide or refer patients to further medical support when necessary.

Design, fabricate, or repair assistive devices or make adaptive changes to equipment or environments, and maintain and promote a positive attitude toward clients and their treatment programs. Teach patients how to deal constructively with their emotions, and conduct therapy sessions designed to enhance interpersonal skills or develop social relationships. Evaluate patients' laboratory and medical records, and collaborate with other healthcare professionals involved in patient care.

Assist anesthesiologists in the administration of anesthesia for surgical and non-surgical procedures, monitor patient status and provide patient care during surgeries, and handle surgical instruments and supplies efficiently. Maintain sanitation and cleanliness of equipment and environments related to patient care, ensuring that all procedures comply with health and safety regulations. Work as part of a team of healthcare professionals to manage patient care during medical procedures and ensure high standards of care are met.
Gather, arrange, or assemble instruments or supplies and determine availability of necessary equipment or supplies for operative procedures.
Obtain or inspect sterile or non-sterile surgical equipment, instruments, or supplies and verify the identity of patient or operative site.
Insert or remove urinary bladder catheters.

Assist maternal patients to find physical positions that will facilitate childbirth and provide comfort and relaxation measures for mothers in labor through interventions such as massage, breathing techniques, hydrotherapy, or music.
Monitor maternal condition during labor by checking vital signs, monitoring uterine contractions, or performing physical examinations.
Coordinate or participate in the positioning of patients, using body stabilizing equipment or protective padding.
Clamp, ligate, or cauterize blood vessels to control bleeding during surgical entry, using hemostatic clamps, suture ligatures, or electrocautery equipment.
Assist members of the surgical team with gowning or gloving and assist in the insertion, positioning, or suturing of closed-wound drainage systems.

Assess skin integrity or other body conditions upon completion of the procedure to determine if damage has occurred from body positioning and adjust and maintain operating room temperature, humidity, or lighting according to surgeon's specifications.
Conduct ongoing prenatal health assessments, tracking changes in physical and emotional health, and develop, implement, or evaluate individualized plans for midwifery care.
Set up or monitor the administration of oxygen or medications, and place devices, such as blood pressure cuffs and other monitoring equipment on patients to monitor vital signs.
Collect specimens for use in laboratory tests and assist in the conduct of client screenings or assessments of language, voice, fluency, articulation, or hearing.
Assist with training sessions to disseminate information regarding equipment or instruments and document clients' progress toward meeting established treatment objectives.
Collect and compile data to document clients' performance or assess program quality.
Assist speech-language pathologists in the remediation or development of speech and language skills and implement treatment plans or protocols as directed by speech-language pathologists.
Monitor changes in legislation and accreditation standards that affect information security or privacy in the computerized healthcare system.
Train medical records staff and manage the department or supervise clerical workers, directing or controlling activities of personnel in the medical records department.
Conduct in-service training sessions, or family and community education programs.
Perform safety checks to verify proper equipment functioning and test or maintain equipment to ensure correct performance.
Collect specimens from patients, using standard medical procedures.
